description_long: This guard stone is a large, roughly rectangular block of weathered gray stone, partially embedded in a patch of earth and grass. Its surface is uneven and pitted, with visible chipping and natural erosion, especially along the edges and corners. The top face is relatively flat but marked by shallow grooves and discoloration, likely from years of exposure to the elements and possible vehicle contact. The sides show a mix of rough, jagged textures and smoother, worn areas, suggesting repeated brushing or scraping. Moss and dirt are present at the base, blending the stone into its natural surroundings. The overall appearance is utilitarian and robust, typical of objects used to protect corners or boundaries in urban environments.
description: A large, weathered gray stone block with a flat, grooved top and rough, chipped sides, partially embedded in earth and grass.
description_view_0: The front view shows the broad, flat face of the stone with visible chipping and a slightly sloped top, blending into grassy earth at the base.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the elongated, rectangular shape and the rough, uneven texture along the length, with grass and dirt visible around the base.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a flatter, more uniform stone surface with shallow grooves and less pronounced chipping, bordered by a grassy patch.
description_view_3: The opposite side view reveals the stone's tapered end and more pronounced surface wear, with visible moss and earth at the base.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the edge of urban streets, near the corners of buildings, driveways, or at the entrance to historical sites to prevent vehicle encroachment and protect structures.
attribute_installation_orientation: Partially embedded in the ground with the flat face exposed above ground, aligned parallel or perpendicular to the edge it is protecting.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible chipping and rounding on edges and corners from vehicle contact and foot traffic; surface erosion and discoloration from prolonged weather exposure; moss and dirt accumulation at the base due to ground contact.
